name: BITTLER RES (MICK & NANETTE)
address: 1654 NW WILD RYE CIRCLE, BEND, OR 97703

fr.recommendations: Enhancing the property's emergency response capabilities could begin with considering methods to provide multiple access points, as this could facilitate more seamless entry and exit for responders. Improving access to the rear of the buildings, potentially by creating or expanding alleys or pathways, might support greater maneuverability and operational flexibility. Managing dense vegetation, particularly around the perimeter and near structures, could improve visibility and reduce obstacles for emergency personnel. Maintaining clear and unobstructed routes within the landscaped areas, as well as ensuring that the front driveway remains free from encroachments, may assist in more effective movement and staging. Lastly, ensuring that perimeter fencing is fitted with clearly accessible and easily identifiable gates could aid responders in navigating the property efficiently during critical operations.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between existing vegetation and the structure, along with managing the vegetation adjacent to the building, could significantly improve defensible space and reduce potential exposure to wildfire. Creating clear zones by trimming or selectively removing vegetation near the structure and along the access route may help minimize fuel continuity, thus supporting better access for firefighters. Furthermore, preserving the open terrain around the property can function as a natural buffer, aiding in reducing the opportunities for fire to advance toward the structure. Regularly checking and maintaining these areas will also sustain long-term wildfire resilience and bolster overall property preparedness. It is advisable to keep the access routes clear and passable to facilitate quick response and suppression operations when necessary.
